slides.title: Your calves are a *second heart*.
slides.sub: Every step squeezes blood back up toward your heart. Here is why that pump matters more as you age.

slides.title: The muscle pump
slides.body: When your calves contract, they *push blood upward* against gravity, helping return it from your legs to your heart with every stride.

slides.title: Easing the load
slides.body: Strong calf contractions share the work of circulation, which *can ease* the demand on your heart so it does not pump alone.

slides.title: Sitting stalls the pump
slides.body: Long stretches of sitting let the pump go quiet. Pooled blood in the legs *is linked to* swelling and sluggish circulation.

slides.title: Veins lose their spring
slides.body: Vein walls and valves soften over time. A strong, active calf pump *may help* protect healthy return flow for decades.

slides.title: Move the pump often
slides.body: Walks, calf raises and breaking up sitting *keep the pump working*. Small movement, repeated through the day, adds up.

slides.title: Keep the *pump moving* daily.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps, movement, sleep and food, then turns them into one daily Health Score so the habits that protect circulation actually stick.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your calves do quiet work every time you stand and walk: pumping blood back up to your heart. 🌿

The muscle pump eases cardiac load, fights pooling from too much sitting, and supports healthy circulation as your veins age. Move often, and it keeps paying off. Save this. 🔖
